channel.title: Posts on ivomarino.com
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on ivomarino.com

item.title: Out-of-Band SSH Tunnel Failover: Why Stability Beats Speed
item.description: Building reliable automatic gateway failover for critical infrastructure recovery access. How naive health checks broke everything, and why a 30-second stable recovery is better than a 5-second flapping failover.

item.title: Kubernetes Pod Autoscaling with KEDA: Queue Depth Beats CPU Every Time
item.description: Kubernetes HPA with CPU metrics fails for queue-based workloads. Learn why queue depth is the right signal, how KEDA makes event-driven autoscaling trivial, and the configuration that reduced processing latency from 45 minutes to 5-15 minutes with zero manual scaling.

item.title: Production Security Updates Without Downtime: Debian, FreeBSD, and OpenBSD
item.description: Security patches can’t wait, but unplanned reboots break production. The update runbook for Debian, FreeBSD, and OpenBSD — with real scripts, hold patterns, and a zero-downtime cluster update sequence.

item.title: From Data Science to Production: MLOps CI/CD Pipeline with Real-time Accuracy Monitoring
item.description: Production machine learning requires more than training good models. This guide covers building a complete MLOps system with Kubernetes, Prometheus monitoring, and automated accuracy checks to catch model drift and prevent silent failures in production.

item.title: ZFS + NFS as Kubernetes Storage: Point-in-Time Recovery Without the Cost
item.description: Kubernetes storage is either ephemeral or expensive. We run a FreeBSD VM with ZFS + NFS in the same VPC as our K8s cluster. Hourly snapshots protect your data. Fast recovery. No vendor lock-in. Here’s how we do it.

item.title: OpenBSD CARP Firewalls in Front of a Private Kubernetes Cluster
item.description: We run two OpenBSD firewalls in CARP HA mode in front of a private Kubernetes cluster. Sub-second failover, full state sync, and pf rules that load-balance across worker nodes.

item.title: Scaling MySQL Beyond Kubernetes: When Backups Become The Blocker
item.description: We ran MySQL in Kubernetes for years until the data scaled from GB to TB. Kubernetes wasn’t the problem—backups were. Kasten (the Kubernetes backup tool) was never designed for databases at our scale. Learn why we moved MySQL off Kubernetes and the backup solution we built to solve this infrastructure challenge.

item.title: 2026 Relaunch: Infrastructure Lessons from 10+ Years Running Production
item.description: Relaunching with a focus on pragmatic infrastructure: backup strategies, Kubernetes autoscaling, and lessons from 10+ years of production operations.
